title: Dip Into National Cheese Fondue Day!
story: Bubbling pots of melted cheese take center stage every April 11 during National Cheese Fondue Day! People gather around fondue pots, dipping chunks of bread, vegetables, and fruit into gooey, delicious cheese mixtures. Restaurants offer special fondue menus, while families dust off their fondue sets at home for cheesy dinner parties. The Swiss tradition of melting cheese became a party favorite worldwide, and today people share photos of their stretchy, golden creations on social media. Some adventurous cheese lovers even try chocolate fondue for dessert, making this holiday doubly sweet!
activity_question: What do people traditionally dip into cheese fondue?
bonus_fact: The word 'fondue' comes from the French word meaning 'to melt.'

title: Harmony in the Hair Shop
story: Four voices blend together in perfect harmony every April 11 on National Barbershop Quartet Day! People gather to listen to these special singing groups who perform without any instruments. Barbershop quartets wear matching outfits and sing old-fashioned songs with lots of "ohs" and "ahs" that make your ears tingle. Some groups even perform in actual barbershops or on street corners, just like singers did long ago.
activity_question: What makes barbershop quartet singing special?
activity_choices: They only sing while getting haircuts
They use microphones shaped like scissors
bonus_fact: The four voice parts are called tenor, lead, baritone, and bass.

title: Going Deep for National Submarine Day
story: Every April 11, National Submarine Day celebrates these amazing underwater vessels that can dive thousands of feet below the ocean surface! People visit submarine museums, watch submarine movies, and share cool submarine facts online. Navy bases often host special tours where families can explore real submarines and learn how sailors live underwater for months at a time. Kids love discovering how submarines use periscopes to see above water and sonar to navigate in the dark depths. Some aquariums even host submarine-themed events with underwater exhibits and hands-on activities that show how these incredible machines work beneath the waves.
activity_question: What do submarines use to see above water while staying underwater?
bonus_fact: Some submarines can stay underwater for over six months without surfacing!

title: Groovy Gadgets Get Their Day
story: Eight-track tapes were chunky music players that ruled cars and homes in the 1970s! On April 11, music lovers celebrate National Eight Track Tape Day by digging out old players, sharing vintage music memories, and posting photos of their collections online. Some people hunt for rare tapes at thrift stores or garage sales. Radio stations might play classic hits that were popular when eight-tracks dominated.
activity_question: What do people often hunt for at thrift stores on this holiday?
bonus_fact: Eight-track tapes were so thick they could barely fit in your hand!
